text: is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是VOA慢速英语健康报道。

text: The World Health Organization declared swine flu as the first international pandemic of the century on June 11th.
zh: 世界卫生组织于6月11日宣布猪流感为本世纪首例国际大流行病。

text: The WHO decided to raise its alert against the new H1N1 flu virus to the highest level after a meeting of health experts in Geneva.
zh: 在日内瓦举行的健康专家会议后，世卫组织决定将针对新型H1N1流感病毒的警戒级别提升至最高级。

text: The announcement means that countries must keep a close watch on the virus.
zh: 这一宣布意味着各国必须密切监测该病毒。

text: WHO Director General Margaret Chan said,
zh: 世卫组织总干事陈冯富珍表示，

text: the world's rich and poor countries must come together to make sure no countries are left behind without help.
zh: 世界上富裕国家和贫穷国家必须携手合作，确保没有任何国家在得不到帮助的情况下被抛下。

text: As of June 11th, 74 countries had reported almost 30 ,000 cases of the H1N1 flu virus.
zh: 截至6月11日，74个国家已报告近3万例H1N1流感病毒病例。

text: 144 deaths were reported.
zh: 已报告144例死亡病例。

text: A World Health Organization official, Keiji Fukuda, said the virus continued to spread in North America.
zh: 世界卫生组织官员福田敬二表示，该病毒在北美地区持续传播。

text: And there were increasing reports from South America as well as from other southern countries.
zh: 而南美洲以及其他南半球国家的报告病例也在增加。

text: The United States has had by far the most confirmed cases.
zh: 迄今为止，美国的确诊病例数目遥遥领先。

text: More than 13 ,000 were reported as of June 8th, including 27 deaths.
zh: 截至6月8日，美国已报告超过1.3万例病例，其中包括27例死亡。

text: The H1N1 outbreak began in Mexico in March.
zh: H1N1疫情于3月在墨西哥爆发。

text: Mexico has reported more than 100 deaths.
zh: 墨西哥已报告超过100例死亡病例。

text: Dr. Fukuda said the public should understand what a pandemic means.
zh: 福田博士表示，公众应了解“大流行”一词的含义。

text: It means that the spread of the virus has continued and that it has become established in at least two areas of the world.
zh: 这意味着病毒的传播仍在持续，并且已经在世界至少两个地区扎根蔓延。

text: He said it does not mean that the severity of the situation has increased or that people are getting seriously sick at higher numbers.
zh: 他表示，这并不意味着疫情的严重程度有所加剧，也不意味着重症病例的数量增多。

text: Dr. Fukuda, an acting assistant director general, said the WHO has been working with the member states to prepare for a pandemic.
zh: 现任代理助理总干事的福田博士说，世卫组织一直在与成员国合作，为应对大流行做准备。

text: Scientists are working on vaccines to protect against the new virus.
zh: 科学家们正在研制针对这种新病毒的疫苗。

text: Governments have been gathering supplies of antiviral drugs.
zh: 各国政府一直在储备抗病毒药物。

text: drugs.
zh: 药物。

text: But public health officials have also had to consider the risks if the public overreacts to a pandemic declaration.
zh: 但公共卫生官员也不得不考虑，如果公众对大流行宣布反应过度会带来什么风险。

text: Dr. Fukuda pointed out that hospitals might quickly fill with people who are worried but not especially sick.
zh: 福田博士指出，医院可能会迅速被那些担心染病但实际并未严重患病的人挤满。

text: Then hospitals might not be able to care for other patients who really do need help.
zh: 这样一来，医院可能无法照顾真正需要帮助的其他病人。

text: The last flu pandemic was in 1968, caused by the so -called Hong Kong flu.
zh: 上一次流感大流行发生在1968年，是由所谓的“香港流感”引起的。

text: Scientists say the common name for the new disease, swine flu, is misleading.
zh: 科学家表示，这种新疾病的俗称“猪流感”具有误导性。

text: The virus combines human, bird, and pig viruses.
zh: 该病毒是人类、禽类和猪流感病毒的组合体。

text: But eating meat from pigs has not been a danger.
zh: 但食用猪肉并不会带来危险。

text: And that's the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 以上就是本期VOA慢速英语健康报道的内容。

text: For more health news, go to voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 欲了解更多健康新闻，请访问voaspecialenglish.com。
